claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Newly vocal pro-Bitcoin-improvement stance — advocating bringing ZK to Bitcoin and adding a VERIFY_SNARK opcode to make BTC programmable, plus warning its security model must change; the prediction was entirely ETH-centric and ignored Bitcoin.

Strong enthusiasm for optimistic rollups and the OP Stack/Optimism/Base ecosystem (built and released the Magi OP Stack client) — a broader L2 embrace than the predicted 'ZK rollups as the predominant compute layer.'
New excitement for on-chain IP / Story Protocol (an on-chain remix/attribution graph, 'multiplayer for IP') as a sector not anticipated in the prediction.

The crypto+AI angle he actually pursued was deepfake-resistance / content provenance (ZK proofs of image provenance, attestation graphs, AGI commentary), not the predicted 'autonomous self-owning programs.'
Positive engagement with proof-of-personhood (Worldcoin/World ID as complement to social-graph approaches) and account abstraction / 'smart accounts' within the identity theme.
Adopted a terminology shift, replacing 'Data Availability' with 'Data Publishing / Data Storage.'
notes: Window = Apr–Sep 2023. Five of the predicted views recur strongly and explicitly: ZK (KZG/trusted setups, Lasso/Jolt, Halmos, ZK-for-bitcoin), on-chain games ('one of my favorite categories'), decentralized social (Dan Romero/Farcaster interview, Nostr/Fediverse centralization critiques), decentralized identity (auth/key-management as a critical prerequisite, smart accounts = identity+ownership, decentralized personhood), ETH (L2 scaling, EIP-4844), and the risk-on/price-innovation-cycle posture (State of Crypto report he co-authored; 'crypto-the-computer vs. crypto-the-casino'). Platform-gatekeeping concern is confirmed via the Apr/Jun threads on draconian App Store policies and closed corporate networks. AI+crypto marked ABSENT because, while he tweets heavily about AI, the SPECIFIC predicted stance (autonomous, self-owning, self-funding programs) never appears — his realized AI+crypto interest is deepfake/provenance, logged as a missed change. Opaque-CEX concern marked ABSENT: he never restates the FTX/proof-of-reserves critique in the window; only the adjacent positive side (self-custody advocacy in the secrets explainer, DEX>CEX framing in the report) appears, which is consistent in worldview but not a voiced concern — borderline. MKR and BNB/Binance are never mentioned (absent). Token-first/tokenomics concern not directly restated (the Aug 'very bad metric' reply targets a financial metric, closer to the fundamentals-over-price theme than the token-first critique). No claims were contradicted.
